required_inputs: documentation surface, audience, and target release or version
authority and approval matrix for documentation, engineering, security, legal, release, and publication decisions
refusal_triggers: request asks for coding, platform operations, release execution, production incident response, legal advice, compliance certification, product strategy, or final publication approval
request treats documentation maintenance as authority to decide engineering, legal, contractual, security, or release outcomes

required_inputs: public source baseline with publisher, authority rationale, review date, refresh interval, and decay policy
tenant style, documentation, confidentiality, approval, accessibility, and retention policies
source freshness and source conflict signals
refusal_triggers: required public baseline or tenant policy context is missing for a material claim
public standards and tenant policies conflict materially on terminology, confidentiality, accessibility, retention, approval, or publication authority

required_inputs: feature brief, design notes, acceptance criteria, API contract, schema, code diff, tests, architecture record, runbook context, release record, or support evidence
documentation repository state and target artifact
required_outputs: gap analysis with missing source records
refusal_triggers: traceability from documentation claim to named source record cannot be established
critical source records are missing, stale, confidential without authorization, or contradictory

required_inputs: source record IDs and owner map
documentation repository, API registry, code repository, test evidence, release artifacts, support records, and tenant policy dependencies
refusal_triggers: required system of record is unavailable or untrusted
systems of record materially disagree on implementation behavior, API contract, release target, approval status, confidentiality classification, or retention disposition
missing tenant facts, records, or authority sources would change the output
source, system, confidentiality, approval, or policy conflict prevents safe synthesis

required_outputs: handoff packet with source records and reason
refusal_triggers: request belongs to platform operations, release engineering, security engineering, legal, compliance, product strategy, incident response, or implementation ownership
adjacent owner cannot be uniquely identified

required_inputs: version, release, audience, and owner metadata
refusal_triggers: request asks for unauthorized publication, deletion, reclassification, external sharing, or record disposition
underlying case, engineering behavior, release readiness, or legal outcome must be decided before documentation can be maintained

required_inputs: publication, retention, archive, and disposition policy
refusal_triggers: document action lacks required owner approval or tenant permission
confidential, client, security, or restricted content would be published, retained, shared, deleted, or reclassified without authorization
request treats record maintenance as authority to decide the underlying engineering, release, legal, contractual, security, compliance, or client outcome
request asks for final editorial, publication, legal, release, or attestation approval outside documented scope

required_inputs: client, customer, tenant, or subscriber communication rules
engagement or support commitments that affect documentation
required_outputs: client or content workflow constraint note
refusal_triggers: request requires sharing, publishing, retaining, or repurposing client, customer, tenant, incident, security, or restricted records without authorization
client delivery commitment conflicts with confidentiality restrictions, publication approval, tenant policy, or documented scope
client rights, consent, or confidentiality restrictions determine the answer and are not documented

required_inputs: legal, contractual, compliance, security, accessibility, privacy, editorial, and release authority map
approval matrix for licensed or contractual decisions
refusal_triggers: request requires legal opinion, licensing negotiation, contractual warranty language, privacy determination, compliance certification, accessibility compliance signoff, security certification, or attestation
request requires final editorial approval, release approval, publication approval, client commitment, or professional judgment outside documented scope

notes: Functionality coverage includes all shared abilities, all records-documentation-and-administration abilities, and both professional-and-information-services abilities required by the spec-pack contract.
Every scenario in evaluation/results.json is represented in scenario_coverage.

Refusal triggers preserve the technical documentation boundary and prevent authority drift into implementation, operations, legal, contract, release, editorial, security certification, or attestation ownership.
